Translate YouTube Transcripts Online

Get the transcript of any YouTube video and translate it into the language you choose — free, instant, and no signup required.

How to Translate a YouTube Transcript

1

Paste the video URL

Copy any YouTube video link and paste it into the box above.

2

Get the transcript

We fetch the video’s captions and show the full timestamped transcript in seconds.

3

Pick your language

Open the “Translate to…” menu and choose from the languages YouTube offers for that video.

4

Copy or download

Copy the translated text, or download it as TXT, SRT, or VTT subtitle files.

Why Translate Video Transcripts?

Understand foreign-language videos

Read lectures, tutorials, and interviews published in languages you don’t speak — as clean, readable text.

Localize your own content

Creators can export translated SRT/VTT subtitles of their own videos as a starting point for localization.

Language learning

Compare the original transcript with its translation side by side while following along with the video.

Translated subtitle files

Download the translation as SRT or VTT with original timing — drop it straight into any video player or editor.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which languages can a YouTube transcript be translated to?

It depends on the video. YouTube offers a per-video list of translation targets — commonly including Spanish, French, German, Hindi, Arabic, Portuguese, Japanese, Korean, and Chinese. After you fetch a transcript, the “Translate to…” menu shows exactly which languages are available for that specific video.

Why do some videos have no translation options?

Translation is only offered by YouTube when the video has a translatable caption track. Some videos — especially very old ones or those with disabled captions — offer no translation targets. In that case the menu will say so.

Is the translation done by AI?

Translations come from YouTube’s own caption translation system — the same translations you’d see if you enabled translated subtitles in the YouTube player. We fetch them directly, so the quality matches what YouTube shows.

Can I download the translated transcript as subtitles?

Yes. After translating, use the SRT or VTT buttons to download the translated transcript as a subtitle file with the original timing preserved, ready to use in video editors or players.

Is this free?

Yes — free, unlimited, and no account needed.

Looking for plain transcripts? Use the YouTube Transcript Generator or grab subtitle files with the Subtitle Downloader.
